bounded_excerpt: 2 Last Revision: February 23, 2026 Guideline Resource Unit Table 1 . Summary of Guideline and Delphi Study Recommendations for Management of Chronic Radiation Dermatitis Author, Year Focus Summary of Recommendations Wong , 20131 (MASCC) Radiation -induced telangiectasia and fibrosis • Weak recommendation for the use of pulse dye laser to achieve greater satisfaction over the visual appearance (Level of evidence III, Recommendation Grade B) ; • insufficient evidence to support a recommendation for or against pentoxifylline and vitamin E for the reduction of fibrosis in standard clinical practice (Level of evidence II, Recommendation Grade B) . Wilson, 20222 (Delphi , international) Chronic radiation dermatitis and fibrosis • Inclusion of laser therapy in the management of chronic radiation dermatitis and fibrosis (96% consensus) • Vascular lasers for telangiectasias and vascular changes (77% consensus) • Fractional ablative laser for fibrosis and induration (75% consensus among those with expertise in laser therapy) • Q‐switched laser for hyperpigmentation (100% consensus among those with expertise in laser therapy) • Fractional ablative laser for skin contractures (90% consensus am

bounded_excerpt: Average time since RT: 7.7 year (SD 4.1) . Telangiectasias started within 1yr of RT for 73% of pts. Radiation dose: 48- 50.4Gy in 24- 28 fractions. Impact measured through Skindex -16, Breast -Q Adverse Effects of Radiation, and HR -QOL administered before and after laser therapy . 16/22 of pts (73%) had 50% clearance , and on average 1.6 treatments were needed to achieve >50% clearance.

bounded_excerpt: Adverse effects reported in 29/189 pts, mostly ear barotrauma. Fibrosis Harpso, 20257 PMID 40542130 Retrospective cohort study (single arm) LOE: V Effectiveness of pentoxifylline and vitamin E on RIF in C and HNC pts N=54 BC and HNC pts w severe and symptomatic RIF BC (n =24): Median age: 62y Median radiation dose: 45.7Gy (2-2.67Gy/fraction) Median time till tx: 31mo HNC (n=30): Median age: 65y Female: 37% Median radiation dose: 67.8Gy (2Gy/fractio) Median time till tx: 42mo • Oral p entoxifylline 400mg and vitamin E (290- 350mg) twice daily BC pts were scored with the LENT -SOMA scale . Median tx duration: BC: 14mo (effect), 6mo (no effect) . HNC: 12mo (effect), 5mo (no effect). Improvement reported by 18/24 (75%) BC pts and 7/30 ( 23%) HNC pts , confirmed clinically as partial or complete regression of RIF in 17/24 BC and 6/30 HNC pts.
quoted_span: Adverse effects reported in 29/189 pts, mostly ear barotrauma. bounded_excerpt: RIF thickness was measured at thickest point with high- frequency US and compared to neighboring normal skin. Pts were also administered VQ -Dermato questionnaire. 13% of pts had grade 2+ AE and stopped pravastatin before 11 mo. Reduction in RIF thickness in 40 pts with ≥ 11 mo pravastin was 17% (SD 39%). Reduction of ≥30% was observed in 15/40 pts (36%). At 12- mo evaluation, RIF severity was decreased in 21/40 pts (50%). No rebound effect was observed after treatment completion.

Griffin, 201910 PMID 31389085 Retrospective cohort study (single arm) LOE: V Effectiveness of lipotransfer to treat RIF and restore volume loss in HNC pts N=38 HNC pts after surgery and RT, with 2- yr DFS, with complaints of RIF and volume defects after RT or surgery + RT/chemo Male: 58% Average age: 51y Primary tumour location mandible: 45% SCC or adenocarcinoma: 46% Sarcoma: 44% Ablative + reconstructive surgery: 97% • Lipotransfer (n=38) Adipose tissue was harvested from pts abdomen and injected using Coleman techniques (single procedure in 44% of pts and multiple procedures in 55% of pts). Improvements rated by pts and clinicians, and included SF -36, POS- head/neck, UW -QOL V4, VAS and DAS24 Mean follow -up: 32 mo

bounded_excerpt: 9 Last Revision: February 23, 2026 Guideline Resource Unit Appendix B: Levels of Evidence • Level I – evidence from at least one large randomized controlled trial (RCT) of good methodological quality with low potential for bias or meta- analyses of RCTs without heterogeneity • Level II – small RCTs, large RCTs with potential bias, meta- analyses including such trials, or RCTs with heterogeneity • Level III – prospective cohort studies • Level IV – retrospective cohort studies or case- control studies • Level V – studies without a control group, case reports, or expert opinions
quoted_span: 9 Last Revision: February 23, 2026 Guideline Resource Unit Appendix B: Levels of Evidence • Level I – evidence from at least one large randomized controlled trial (RCT) of good methodological quality with low potential for bias or meta- analyses of RCTs without heterogeneity • Level II – small RCTs, large RCTs with potential bias, meta- analyses including such trials, or RCTs with heterogeneity • Level III – prospective cohort studies • Level IV – retrospective cohort studies or case- control studies • Level V – studies without a control group, case reports, or expert opinions
